body.title: CLOSED: activate() CTE propagation via interpretive_edges
body.result: activate() propagation replaced with CTE on interpretive_edges; 7 tests pass
body.description:
Replace the bespoke Python recursion in activate() with a `WITH RECURSIVE` CTE against clan.interpretive_edges. CTE traverses edges up to max_depth=3, accumulating propagation weights. Postgres UNION ALL semantics handle cycle detection naturally (visited-set logic moves to DB). Bulk UPDATE after traversal.

CTE shape:
```sql
WITH RECURSIVE prop(memory_id, depth, score) AS (
  SELECT :root_id, 0, :initial_score
  UNION ALL
  SELECT e.target_id, p.depth+1, p.score * :decay * e.weight
  FROM prop p JOIN clan.interpretive_edges e ON e.source_id=p.memory_id
  WHERE p.depth < 3 AND p.score * :decay * e.weight >= 0.05
)
SELECT memory_id, MAX(score) FROM prop GROUP BY memory_id
```
Then bulk UPDATE. Same external interface — activate() signature unchanged.

**Affected files:** `wild_igor/igor/cognition/activate.py` (replace Python propagation with CTE)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; psycopg2 direct; max_depth + min_score remain kwargs (passed as params); no schema changes (interpretive_edges already exists)
**Scope boundary:** IN — propagation implementation only; same activate() interface. OUT — no new columns, no new tables.
**Test plan:** same test coverage as T-igor-activate-primitive; verify CTE stops at depth 3; verify min_score cutoff; verify cycle in edge graph doesn't loop infinitely
